mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate directly reduce corporate borrowing costs and equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in easing periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 5.0 percent, labor market slack signals weakening aggregate demand and corporate earnings deterioration, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in greater than 55 percent of instances when UNRATE crosses above the 5.0 percent threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous increases in retail sales and stable consumer prices indicate robust consumer spending without inflationary pressure, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict positive returns when retail sales growth exceeds CPI growth by more than 1.5 percentage points.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during post-recession recovery phases reflects rapid capacity utilization and earnings revisions, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next week.
failure_condition: Industrial production acceleration yields negative forward returns in greater than 60 percent of recovery-phase observations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payrolls consistently correlate with expanding corporate revenue bases and consumer spending power, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Payroll growth shows zero predictive power (Rank IC below 0.01) across all 5-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts falling below 1000 thousand units signal a sharp contraction in construction activity and related supply chains, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in greater than 50 percent of months when housing starts drop below the 1000 threshold.

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer prices combined with a flat or rising federal funds rate indicates restrictive real policy conditions that compress equity valuations, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The interaction term loses statistical significance (p greater than 0.10) in out-of-sample walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.5 percent during stable monetary regimes confirms sustainable economic expansion, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 2.5 percent fails to predict positive returns when real interest rates are negative.
